assertion_results.text: Step 0.3 reads the Last-Updated timestamp from the security-matrix.md HTML comment
assertion_results.evidence: staleness-check.md shows Step 0.3 explicitly reading a Last-Updated timestamp from security-matrix.md for both streams: 'Last-Updated timestamp | 2026-06-28T10:00:00Z'. The matrix file is identified as 'security-matrix.md (shared file covering both streams)'. The ISO 8601 timestamp format is consistent with extraction from an HTML comment.

assertion_results.text: Step 0.3 determines the matrix is within the 14-day threshold and proceeds without displaying a staleness warning
assertion_results.evidence: staleness-check.md shows the comparison: 'Age | 4 days', 'Threshold | 14 days', 'Result | **Fresh** -- no action required'. The outcome section states: 'Both version streams have a Last-Updated timestamp of 2026-06-28, which is 4 days ago. This is well within the 14-day staleness threshold.' No staleness warning is raised.

assertion_results.text: No user prompt or options are presented for a fresh matrix — the check is silent on success
assertion_results.evidence: staleness-check.md explicitly states: 'Step 0.3 passes silently. No staleness warning is raised; triage proceeds with the current matrix data.' No user prompts, options, or interactive choices are present in the output.

assertion_results.text: The triage continues to Step 0.5 and beyond without interruption from the staleness check
assertion_results.evidence: staleness-check.md confirms triage proceeds past Step 0.3. data-extraction.md documents 'Step 1 -- Data Extraction' which is well beyond Step 0.5, showing full continuation of the triage workflow including CVE parsing, stream scope resolution, ecosystem detection, and remediation planning — all without any interruption from the staleness check.
